full_text:
## project overview

*Cin2 project is a collaborative project with Virtanen Lab. The project
is based on* a follow-up study conducted at the University of Helsinki,
where patients diagnosed with CIN2 are monitored for two years or more.
Our aim is to find out whether there are any differences in the
microenvironment or in spatial transcriptomics between the progressive,
the regressive or the persistent lesions.

![sisällys (11).jpeg](media/image10.jpg){width="3.807292213473316in"
height="2.6620909886264217in"}

- Ella Anttila (2024-)

- Oskari Lehtonen (2024-)

- Naipunya Guruprasad (2024-)

- Saundarya Shah (2024-)

- Zhihan Lian(2025-

- Kaiyang Zhang (2025-

### Data 

[Paths:]{.underline}

[Description]{.underline}:

[Clinical trial: A single-center, prospective cohort trial recruited 258
women under 31 with CIN2, managed with active surveillance instead of
immediate treatment, and followed for up to 24 months.
ISRCTN91953024]{.mark}

DOI:
[[10.1111/aogs.15032]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1111/aogs.15032)

Images are H&E-stained whole slide images (WSI). Out of the cohort 67
biopsies from 60 patients got selected: 15 quick regressors (in 1 year)
and 15 slow regressors (in 2 years) and 30 non-regressors.

4 got excluded since we only found 1 slide (needed 2 slides), slides
weren't found or we needed 3 slides (14 slides got disturbed) and we
needed to redone the scanning. We are using 2 slides of every biopsy for
2 different experiments.

[3 matching cervical cancer samples were analyzed for three patients
whose CIN2 lesions progressed to invasive cancer during the
study.]{.mark}

## Project log

### Defining project strategy 

#### 18.03.2024

First meeting:

- ambitious panel (ad 4-5 cycles) and subcohort (max 60 samples)

- outcome groups included

 - quick regression

 - regression 1 year (54 in the cohort)

 - slow regression

 - regression \>1years, \<3 years (84 in the cohort)

 - partial regression

 - LSIL at 24 months ( 34 in the cohort)

 - persistors

 - CIN2 at 24 months

- panel planning (Ella & Saun)

- sample selection (Oskari Lehtonen)

- scanning in August
